questions.q: What historic distinction is Kalpana Chawla best known for?
options: First woman of Indian origin to go to space
First person to walk on the Moon
First woman to command the International Space Station
questions.explanation: Kalpana Chawla became the first woman of Indian origin to fly to space, aboard the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1997.

questions.q: Which space agency did Kalpana Chawla fly with as an astronaut?
questions.explanation: Chawla was a NASA astronaut, joining the U.S. space agency in 1994 and flying on two Space Shuttle missions.

questions.q: Aboard which Space Shuttle did Kalpana Chawla die in 2003?
questions.explanation: Chawla died aboard the Space Shuttle Columbia, which disintegrated during re-entry on February 1, 2003.

questions.q: What was Kalpana Chawla's professional field of training?
questions.explanation: Chawla was an aerospace engineer, holding advanced degrees and a doctorate in the field before becoming an astronaut.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Kalpana Chawla born?
questions.explanation: Chawla was born in Karnal, in the state of Haryana, India.

questions.q: In which city of Haryana was Kalpana Chawla born?
questions.explanation: Kalpana Chawla was born in Karnal, Haryana.

questions.q: In what year did the Space Shuttle Columbia disaster that claimed Chawla's life occur?
questions.explanation: The Columbia disaster occurred on February 1, 2003, during the shuttle's re-entry into Earth's atmosphere.

questions.q: How many spaceflight missions did Kalpana Chawla complete in her career?
questions.explanation: Chawla flew two Space Shuttle missions: STS-87 in 1997 and STS-107 in 2003.

questions.q: Which country's citizenship did Kalpana Chawla hold at the time of her NASA career?
questions.explanation: Chawla became a naturalized U.S. citizen, which made her eligible to join NASA's astronaut corps.

questions.q: On her missions, the vehicle Kalpana Chawla flew was a NASA Space Shuttle. What kind of spacecraft is that?
questions.explanation: The Space Shuttle was a partially reusable spacecraft with a winged orbiter that launched vertically and landed like a glider.

questions.q: On which Space Shuttle mission did Kalpana Chawla make her first spaceflight in 1997?
questions.explanation: Chawla's first spaceflight was STS-87 aboard Columbia in November 1997.

questions.q: What was the designation of Kalpana Chawla's final, fatal mission in 2003?
questions.explanation: Her second and final mission was STS-107, which ended in the Columbia disaster on February 1, 2003.

questions.q: What role did Kalpana Chawla serve in on her spaceflights?
questions.explanation: Chawla flew as a mission specialist, responsible for operating payloads and conducting experiments.

questions.q: During the STS-87 mission in 1997, Chawla was responsible for operating which piece of equipment to deploy a satellite?
questions.explanation: On STS-87 Chawla operated the shuttle's robotic arm (the Remote Manipulator System) to deploy the Spartan satellite.

questions.q: From which Indian institution did Kalpana Chawla earn her bachelor's degree in aeronautical engineering?
questions.explanation: Chawla earned her Bachelor of Engineering in aeronautical engineering from Punjab Engineering College in Chandigarh.

questions.q: From which U.S. university did Kalpana Chawla earn her doctorate (PhD)?
questions.explanation: Chawla earned her PhD in aerospace engineering from the University of Colorado Boulder in 1988.

questions.q: At which Texas university did Kalpana Chawla earn her first master's degree in aerospace engineering?
questions.explanation: Chawla earned a Master of Science in aerospace engineering from the University of Texas at Arlington in 1984.

questions.q: In which year was Kalpana Chawla selected by NASA to join its astronaut corps?
questions.explanation: Chawla was selected as a NASA astronaut candidate in 1994 and began training in 1995.

questions.q: Before becoming an astronaut, Kalpana Chawla worked as a researcher at which NASA facility, studying fluid dynamics?
questions.explanation: Chawla worked at NASA Ames Research Center on computational fluid dynamics before joining the astronaut corps.

questions.q: What was the primary focus of the STS-107 mission on which Chawla died?
options: Docking with the International Space Station
questions.explanation: STS-107 was a dedicated science mission with around 80 experiments in microgravity, conducted in a Spacehab module.

questions.q: How many crew members in total were lost in the Space Shuttle Columbia disaster of 2003?
questions.explanation: All seven crew members aboard STS-107, including Kalpana Chawla, were lost in the Columbia disaster.

questions.q: Kalpana Chawla developed an early passion for aviation. What did she train to fly before becoming an astronaut?
options: Aircraft and gliders as a certified pilot
questions.explanation: Chawla was a certified flight instructor and held pilot licenses for single- and multi-engine airplanes, seaplanes, and gliders.

questions.q: What was the direct technical cause of the Space Shuttle Columbia disaster identified by investigators?
options: A collision with a meteoroid in orbit
Foam insulation damaging the wing's heat shield at launch
A failure of the landing gear
questions.explanation: A piece of foam insulation broke off the external tank during launch and struck the left wing, damaging the thermal protection that failed during re-entry.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Kalpana Chawla's first mission, STS-87, last?
questions.explanation: STS-87 lasted roughly 16 days in November 1997, during which Columbia orbited Earth more than 250 times.

questions.q: Which board was established to investigate the loss of Columbia and Kalpana Chawla's crew?
questions.explanation: The Columbia Accident Investigation Board (CAIB) investigated the 2003 disaster, while the earlier Rogers Commission had probed the 1986 Challenger accident.

questions.q: ISRO honored Kalpana Chawla by naming the first satellite of which weather series after her?
questions.explanation: India's MetSat-1 meteorological satellite was renamed Kalpana-1 in her honor after the Columbia disaster.

questions.q: On her final mission STS-107, which Space Shuttle orbiter did Kalpana Chawla fly aboard?
questions.explanation: Both of Chawla's missions, STS-87 and STS-107, flew aboard Columbia, the oldest of NASA's shuttle orbiters.

questions.q: NASA named a supercomputer after Kalpana Chawla. At which research center was this 'Kalpana' supercomputer installed?
questions.explanation: NASA dedicated a supercomputer named 'Kalpana' at the Ames Research Center, where she had earlier worked, in her memory.

questions.q: Which decoration was Kalpana Chawla posthumously awarded by the United States for her spaceflight service?
options: The Congressional Space Medal of Honor
questions.explanation: Chawla was posthumously awarded the Congressional Space Medal of Honor, along with the NASA Space Flight Medal and the NASA Distinguished Service Medal.

questions.q: A Northrop Grumman Cygnus cargo spacecraft launched to the ISS in 2020 was named in tribute to Kalpana Chawla. What does this reflect?
options: The company's tradition of naming Cygnus craft after fallen space pioneers
She was the first to dock a Cygnus
questions.explanation: Northrop Grumman names each Cygnus resupply spacecraft after a person who advanced human spaceflight, and the NG-14 vehicle was christened the 'S.S. Kalpana Chawla'.
